---
title: "Patch Enrichment"
sidebar: scenarios
---

Patch enrichment is explicit. A patch carries local metadata and a `data_source_id`; the inventory supplies richer context when the user requests it.

## Identifier

```text
XX.GARDEN..RAW
```

This resolves:

- network `XX`
- fiber array `GARDEN`
- empty location code
- acquisition `RAW`

## Proposal Sketch

```python
patch = patch.update_attrs(data_source_id="XX.GARDEN..RAW")

patch = patch.add_inventory_attrs(
    inventory,
    attrs=(
        "acquisition_sample_rate",
        "gauge_length",
        "interrogator.model",
    ),
)
```

The enriched patch can carry selected acquisition and instrument context without turning the inventory into patch-local state.

## Boundary

`PatchAttrs` remains the right place for patch-local metadata:

- processing `history`
- patch `tag`
- local unit changes after processing
- source file hints
- the `data_source_id` used to resolve inventory context

The inventory remains the right place for durable acquisition, geometry, coupling, and optical-path context.
